pattern: A min-heap of non-full stack indices for pushes plus trimming of trailing empty stacks for pops keeps both leftmost-insert and rightmost-remove fast.
hints: Push always targets the leftmost stack with room; that is a minimum-index query over the set of non-full stacks.
Pop always targets the rightmost non-empty stack, so keep dropping empty trailing stacks until you reach one with elements.
Maintain a min-heap of available (non-full) stack indices for push, and after a popAtStack reopens room, add that index back; lazily discard stale or out-of-range heap entries.

edge_inputs.description: interleaved pushes, middle pops, and right-end pops
capacity one makes every stack hold a single value
pop on an all-empty structure returns minus one
a middle pop reopens room so the next push refills the gap
popAtStack on an empty given index returns minus one

stress.description: randomized push, pop, and popAtStack mix against a small capacity
stress.note: popAtStack indices stay near the populated range so both hits and misses occur
